title: Thirst.
content:
We thirst at first, -- 't is Nature's act;
    And later, when we die,
    A little water supplicate
    Of fingers going by.

    It intimates the finer want,
    Whose adequate supply
    Is that great water in the west
    Termed immortality.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Thirst.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
